2605.06454.title: ORTHOBO: Orthogonal Bayesian Hyperparameter Optimization
2605.06454.paper_summary: Bayesian optimization is widely used for hyperparameter optimization when model evaluations are expensive; however, noisy acquisition estimates can lead to unstable decisions. We identify acquisition estimation noise as a failure mode that was previously overlooked: even when the surrogate model and acquisition target are correctly specified, finite-sample Monte Carlo error can perturb acquisition values. This can, in turn, flip candidate rankings and lead to suboptimal BO decisions. As a remedy, we aim at variance reduction and propose an orthogonal acquisition estimator that subtracts an optimally weighted score-function control variate, which yields an acquisition residual orthogonal to posterior score directions and which thus reduces Monte Carlo variance. We further introduce OrthoBO: a Bayesian optimization framework that combines our orthogonal acquisition estimator with ensemble surrogates and an outer log transformation. We show theoretically that our estimator preserves the target, leads to variance reduction, and improves pairwise ranking stability. We further verify the theoretical properties of OrthoBO through numerical experiments where our framework reduces acquisition estimation variance, stabilizes candidate rankings, and achieves strong performance. We also demonstrate the downstream utility of OrthoBO in hyperparameter optimization for neural network training and fine-tuning.
2605.06454.paper_summary_zh: 貝葉斯優化在模型評估成本高昂時廣泛用於超參數優化；然而，噪聲的獲取估計可能導致不穩定的決策。<lb/>我們將獲取估計噪聲識別為一種之前被忽視的失效模式：即使代理模型和獲取目標正確指定，有限樣本的蒙特卡羅誤差也可能擾動獲取值。<lb/>這可能反過來翻轉候選排名並導致次優的貝葉斯優化決策。<lb/>作為補救措施，我們旨在減少方差，並提出一種正交獲取估計器，該估計器減去一個最佳加權的得分函數控制變量，從而產生一個與後驗得分方向正交的獲取殘差，進而減少蒙特卡羅方差。<lb/>我們進一步介紹OrthoBO：一個將我們的正交獲取估計器與集成代理和外部對數變換相結合的貝葉斯優化框架。<lb/>我們理論上證明了我們的估計器保持目標，導致方差減少，並改善成對排名的穩定性。<lb/>我們還通過數值實驗驗證了OrthoBO的理論特性，該框架減少了獲取估計的方差，穩定了候選排名，並取得了良好的性能。<lb/>我們還展示了OrthoBO在神經網絡訓練和微調的超參數優化中的下游效用。
2605.06454.author: Maresa Schröder et.al.
2605.06454.authors: Maresa Schröder, Pascal Janetzky, Michael Klar, Stefan Feuerriegel
